_metadata.description: Golden labeled set for F3 impact classification eval (Day 26).
_metadata.labeling_methodology: v1 — Claude-as-labeler, NOT a compliance SME. Pairs sampled from data/f3_indexes/impact_results.json, stratified to cover: (a) clear true positives, (b) clear true negatives, (c) the Day 25 'Equal Credit Opportunity Act (Regulation B) / Agency Information Collection Activities over-matches generic compliance language across unrelated policies' finding, and (d) borderline mid-range scores. Each label includes a one-line rationale. PENDING: review by a compliance officer (Sarah persona) before this set is treated as production ground truth — SR 11-7 requires human validation of model eval data, not just the model.
_metadata.dense_score_snapshot_note: dense_score values are a snapshot from the Day 25 run of matches.json. The eval pipeline re-looks-up dense_score from the current matches.json at eval time — if re-running build_indexes/matcher changes embeddings/chunking, scores may drift slightly from this snapshot.

pairs.rationale: Section is literally about ECOA/Reg B prohibited bases. Direct, substantive match.
Discouragement is a named Reg B doctrine (the regulation text even cites the 7th Circuit discouragement case). Direct match.
SPCPs are a Reg B-defined exception to anti-discrimination rules. Direct match, highest dense_score in the corpus.
ECOA fair-lending training content must reflect current Reg B requirements. Direct match.

pairs.regulation_title: Small Business Lending Under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act
pairs.rationale: Section 1071 (small business lending data collection) IS the ECOA small-business-lending rule. Exact topical match.
A section listing the policy's governing regulations should flag a new/updated Reg B publication as directly relevant.
Annual fair lending analysis is performed against ECOA/Reg B standards. Direct, substantive match.

pairs.regulation_title: Small Business Lending Under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act
pairs.rationale: A section about anticipated ECOA requirements should flag the small-business-lending (1071) rule as directly relevant.
BSA §10.2 covers penalties for BSA/AML violations. Reg B is an unrelated statute — match is driven by shared generic 'penalties/consequences' language, not substance.
Good-faith fee tolerances are a TRID/RESPA concept with no ECOA/Reg B counterpart. Generic regulatory-language overlap only.
Closing Disclosure timing is a TRID requirement, not Reg B. Some loose conceptual overlap around 'disclosure timing rules' generally, but not substantive — low, not high.
TRID compliance testing tests TRID requirements. Reg B could be a tangential cross-reference in a real compliance test plan, but this is not a direct match.
CDD information-collection practices intersect with Reg B's rules on what can be asked/recorded during account opening — real but partial overlap, not a core ECOA match.

pairs.regulation_title: Agency Information Collection Activities: Comment Request
pairs.rationale: This regulation is a generic CFPB Federal Register notice about an information-collection comment period — no substantive connection to a BSA policy's purpose statement. Boilerplate Federal Register language drives the match.

pairs.regulation_title: Agency Information Collection Activities: Comment Request
pairs.rationale: Same generic Federal Register notice as pair 14 — no substantive link to BSA high-risk customer categorization.

pairs.regulation_title: Agency Information Collection Activities: Comment Request
pairs.rationale: Both concern regulatory 'notices', but this is a CFPB info-collection notice, not adverse-action notice content under Reg B. Loose thematic overlap only — low, not high.

pairs.regulation_title: Agencies issue host state loan-to-deposit ratios
pairs.rationale: Host-state loan-to-deposit ratios concern interstate branching, completely unrelated to BSA/AML program scope. Clear true negative.
ECOA/Reg B has no bearing on a BSA/AML program's purpose statement. Clear true negative.
Both involve 'remediation of compliance violations' as a general concept, but TRID cure provisions and Reg B are otherwise unrelated. Low is a fair call.
Structuring (breaking transactions to evade CTR thresholds) is a BSA/AML detection topic with zero ECOA relevance.
Pricing-discrimination consistency is a core ECOA fair-lending concern — this is a true positive that the classifier UNDER-scored (predicted 'low'), not an over-match.

pairs.section_title: Four Pillars of the BSA/AML Program
pairs.rationale: The 'four pillars' BSA program structure has no Reg B counterpart. True negative.
Loan Estimate triggering events are a TRID-specific concept (application definition under Reg Z/RESPA-TILA), unrelated to ECOA.
Post-consummation disclosure corrections are TRID-specific. Generic 'changes/compliance' language overlap with Reg B only.

pairs.regulation_title: Federal Reserve Board requests public comment on a proposal to establish a "payment account"
pairs.rationale: OFAC/sanctions screening requirements are unrelated to a Fed proposal about Reserve Bank payment accounts. Generic Fed-press-release language overlap only.
HMDA/Reg B demographic data collection requirements are a direct ECOA topic — this is a true positive UNDER-scored by the classifier (predicted 'medium').
Fair-lending training requirements directly derive from ECOA/Reg B obligations — true positive under-scored by the classifier (predicted 'medium').
High-risk customer categorization can raise fair-lending/disparate-impact concerns under Reg B if categories correlate with prohibited bases — real but secondary overlap.
LOS configuration for TRID disclosure forms has no direct Reg B requirement. Generic 'system/compliance configuration' language overlap only.
A TRID policy's purpose statement and ECOA/Reg B share generic 'consumer protection / disclosure' regulatory framing, but no substantive overlap. Low, not high.
